tomodachiLifePersonalityGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Personality Guide
tomodachiLifePersonalityGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life uses four editable traits during Mii creation to place each resident into one of 16 personalities. Grouped into Easygoing, Outgoing, Independent, and Confident, personalities affect social chemistry, dialogue flavor, and key social outcomes on your island.

steps.title: How the personality quiz works
steps.description: The personality chart is built from Movement, Speech, Expressiveness, and Attitude. The Overall row is cosmetic and does not change the final personality result. Tomodachi Life keeps the same 16 personality types used in Tomodachi Collection.

steps.title: Easygoing personalities
steps.description: Types: Softie, Optimist, Buddy, Dreamer. Group tone: relaxed, open-minded, empathetic. Softie is emotional and highly empathetic. Optimist stays upbeat. Buddy puts friends first. Dreamer is idealistic and romantic.

steps.title: Outgoing personalities
steps.description: Types: Trendsetter, Entertainer, Charmer, Leader. Group tone: social, charming, energetic. Trendsetter is stylish and socially adaptable. Entertainer loves attention. Charmer gets along with almost anyone. Leader is assertive and tends to take charge.

steps.title: Independent personalities
steps.description: Types: Free Spirit, Artist, Lone Wolf, Thinker. Group tone: self-sufficient, individual, introspective. Free Spirit follows personal values. Artist is quiet and creative. Lone Wolf keeps emotions private. Thinker is reflective and analytical.

steps.title: Confident personalities
steps.description: Types: Designer, Adventurer, Brainiac, Go-Getter. Group tone: organized, motivated, focused. Designer is polished and goal-oriented. Adventurer seeks new challenges. Brainiac is highly intelligent. Go-Getter pushes ahead with confidence.

quickTips: Miis with similar personalities tend to like each other more.
A personality profile becomes viewable once your island has at least one resident with that personality.
Personalities affect dialogue flavor, social chemistry, and some romance reactions.
You can check a Mii's personality group in their profile at any time.

tomodachiLifeQRCodes.title: Tomodachi Life QR Codes
tomodachiLifeQRCodes.intro: QR codes let you add established characters, community creations, and ready-made Miis without rebuilding them by hand. The best QR resources combine large Mii archives with clear import instructions and curated packs you can scan immediately.

cards.name: Official Mii Archive on MiiWiki
cards.description: MiiWiki's QR code category is an official-created Mii archive with over 1,015 total QR files, including Aerith, Aonuma, Barret, and many more. Best for broad discovery when you want a large back catalog.

cards.name: Nintendo Life Team Pack
cards.description: Nintendo Life published a ready-to-scan staff pack for Tomodachi Life, generated inside the game itself. Carries over clothing, room décor, catchphrases, personality traits, voice, and original-island information.

cards.name: Crystal Dreams QR Collection
cards.description: Crystal Dreams keeps a long-running Tomodachi Life QR page that works well as a community browse source. Especially useful when you want fan-made picks instead of a strict official archive.

cards.name: QR Import Flow
cards.description: In Mii Maker: open QR Code/Image Options, tap Scan QR Code, align the code, then save the Mii. In Tomodachi Life: go to Town Hall, choose New Mii, then transfer the saved Mii from Mii Maker.

milestones: Scan your first Mii QR code

tomodachiLifeBeginnerGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Beginner Guide
tomodachiLifeBeginnerGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life feels random at first, but the early game has a readable loop: add residents, solve problem bubbles, unlock facilities, level up Miis, and let relationships snowball into bigger events.

items.name: Start with a balanced resident pool
items.description: Create your first Miis and keep expanding early. Aim for at least 1 male and 1 female Mii quickly. Many early buildings require a gender mix, so a balanced cast speeds up progress.

items.name: Solve black-bubble problems first
items.description: Problem bubbles are the main progression engine. At 5 solved problems with 1 male + 1 female Mii, the Clothing Store opens. At 10 problems with 2 male + 2 female, the Hat Store opens.

items.name: Use money and shops to stabilize
items.description: Shops give you more ways to raise happiness, solve requests, and shape each resident. Save money early so the Interiors Store opens after two Miis save $200 or more.

items.name: Level up Miis as soon as possible
items.description: Every level-up unlocks rewards: songs open the Concert Hall, gifts open the Observation Tower. Each reward deepens your island's sandbox and creates new social events.

items.name: Push into island-wide systems
items.description: At 5 Miis and 15 problems: Rankings Board opens. At 7 Miis with 3+ of each gender: Mii News opens. At 2+2 Miis and 30 problems: Compatibility Tester unlocks.

items.name: Let friendships turn into romance
items.description: A love confession unlocks the Park and Café. A wedding unlocks Mii Homes. Keep solving relationship problems to create more chances for confessions, dates, and marriages.

tomodachiLifeCompatibilityGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Compatibility Guide
tomodachiLifeCompatibilityGuide.intro: Compatibility in Tomodachi Life is part forecast tool and part relationship planner. It gives you a fast way to see who is likely to mesh well, which pairs qualify for romance checks, and what status ladder a relationship can climb.

solutions.name: Compatibility Tester
solutions.description: Unlocks when you have 2 male Miis, 2 female Miis, and 30 solved problems. This is the first real tool for planning friendships and romance instead of waiting blindly.

solutions.name: Birthday Requirement
solutions.description: Both Miis need full birthdays saved in their profile. Without full birthdays, compatibility cannot be tested. Set them early for residents you care about.

solutions.name: Friendship Forecast
solutions.description: Used for same-gender pairs, family pairs, or different age group opposite-gender pairs. Shows the next week's Work/Study, Chats, and Leisure outlook.

solutions.name: Romance Forecast
solutions.description: Used for opposite-gender pairs in the same age group. Adds the Love forecast alongside other weekly categories. Best for choosing which pairings to push toward confessions and marriage.

managementTips: Forecast scale runs from full sun (best) to full storm (worst).
Miis with similar personalities tend to like each other more.
Travel tickets can raise a relationship by one or two levels automatically.
A rejected confession can drop both sides by as many as three levels.
Check compatibility before pushing romance — failed confessions are costly.

tomodachiLifeRelationshipGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Relationship Guide
tomodachiLifeRelationshipGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life runs on hidden social momentum. Every friendship, romance, and family bond sits on a visible status ladder that directly affects whether Miis become best friends, start dating, propose, fight, or break up.

cards.name: Relationship Categories
cards.description: The game tracks seven main categories: Spouse, Family, Siblings, Sweetheart, Best Friend, Friends, and Ex-Sweethearts/Ex-Spouses. Each category uses its own wording for relationship strength.

cards.name: Friendship Ladder
cards.description: Strongest to weakest (NA version): Best bud → Good buddy → Great pal → Good pal → Getting along OK → Kinda getting along → Not getting along. This is the main ladder to watch for ordinary social bonds.

cards.name: Romance and Marriage Signals
cards.description: Strong Sweetheart statuses include Let's get married!, Super in love, Totally in love, Very much in love. Strong Spouse statuses include Soul mate, Super happy, Totally happy, Very happy.

cards.name: Ways to Raise a Relationship
cards.description: Positive relationship advice, dates, and travel tickets are the safest ways to improve bonds. Miis in the same personality group generally get along more easily.

highlights: Tell worried Miis they are great together
Use travel tickets to boost bond levels
Keep personality groups in mind when planning friendships

tomodachiLifeMarriageGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Marriage Guide
tomodachiLifeMarriageGuide.intro: Marriage in Tomodachi Life is not a random cutscene you simply wait for. A dating pair must stay stable long enough to trigger a proposal request, then you help with a timing-based minigame, and the couple moves into Mii Homes.

regions.name: Keep the couple stable
regions.description: A marriage request usually appears after the pair has been together for some time. Proposal timing can range from months to, very rarely, under an hour.

regions.name: Accept the proposal request
regions.description: When one sweetheart says they want to propose, choose to help instead of putting it off. Saying Not right now can cause the request to disappear.

regions.name: Choose the proposal setup
regions.description: Choose where the proposal happens: Fireworks show, Train station, Amusement park, or Fancy restaurant. Each location has its own interruptions.

regions.name: Win the proposal minigame
regions.description: Tap the heart button only when the partner is thinking about the proposer. You must get the timing right four times. Three mistakes and the proposal fails.

regions.name: Watch the wedding and honeymoon
regions.description: After the wedding, the couple goes on a honeymoon in a random country. They return with a souvenir and settle in a newly built house in Mii Homes.

regions.name: Unlock married-life outcomes
regions.description: Marriage opens the family layer of the game. A married couple can later ask for a baby if the baby setting is enabled. Divorce is still possible after marriage.

tomodachiLifeBabyGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Baby Guide
tomodachiLifeBabyGuide.intro: Babies only appear after a couple is already married and living in Mii Homes. The player controls whether births are allowed, how the baby is customized, how the child is raised, and whether they stay on the island or leave as a traveler.

creatures.name: Turn baby births on
creatures.description: Babies can only happen if baby births are enabled in Town Hall and the married couple has a positive relationship. The request appears as a love problem from one member.

creatures.name: Accept the baby request
creatures.description: If approved, the baby arrives about one to two days later. A couple can have multiple children but can only actively raise one child at home at a time.

creatures.name: Customize the newborn
creatures.description: Choose Boy, Girl, or random gender. The baby's appearance is a mix of both parents' features. Set the personality like Dad, like Mom, or random.

creatures.name: Follow the six-day growth cycle
creatures.description: Day 1: newborn in arms. Day 2: crawling. Day 3: hair grows. Day 4: toddler stage. Day 5: walks and speaks. Day 6: fully grown and ready to leave.

creatures.name: Use the right babysitting actions
creatures.description: Day 1: rhythmic rocking. Days 2 to 3: head pats, belly tickles, peekaboo. Days 4 to 5: circular motions to lift and spin in the backyard. Parents do not call after 8 PM.

creatures.name: Choose resident or traveler
creatures.description: On day six, choose whether the grown child becomes an island resident or a traveler at the port for StreetPass boats. You get one more chance to edit their profile.

creatures.name: Family restrictions and records
creatures.description: Neither parent can be deleted until the child moves out. Married Miis do not fight or divorce while raising a child. The family album in Town Hall can hold up to 50 albums.

tomodachiLifeFoodGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Food Guide
tomodachiLifeFoodGuide.intro: Food is one of the most repeated systems in Tomodachi Life. It levels Miis, fills hunger, reveals favorite and hated foods, cures certain problems, and supports completion goals through the Town Hall collection list.

items.name: Food Mart Basics
items.description: The Food Mart is the first shop unlocked. Its stock rotates daily. Previously purchased foods are grouped into main dishes, side dishes, desserts, and beverages.

items.name: Daily Specials
items.description: Each day the shop offers 5 Daily Specials randomly from foods you have not bought yet. Once purchased, they are added to the regular In Stock list permanently.

items.name: Food Categories
items.description: Main dishes: 43 total (most filling). Side dishes: 83 total. Desserts: 81 total. Beverages: 24 total (least filling). Use mains when hunger is low.

items.name: Reaction Tiers
items.description: Super All-Time Favorite launches the Mii into space. All-Time Favorite triggers a dance. Worst causes a major happiness drop. Worst Ever empties the happiness bar completely.

items.name: Special Foods
items.description: Special foods always trigger a very positive response and award the largest amount of money and happiness. They never appear as favorites or worst-ever reactions.

items.name: Trash Food
items.description: Trash food costs $0.20 and is always disliked. It cannot be bought from the Food Mart. The Tomodachi Life trash foods are: Ruined meal, Moldy Bread, Banana Peel, and Spoiled Milk.

tomodachiLifeGiftsGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Gifts Guide
tomodachiLifeGiftsGuide.intro: In Tomodachi Life, level-up gifts are different from normal consumable gifts. A Mii can own up to 8 level-up gifts at once. The non-Japanese versions include 28 total options covering solo scenes, group activities, and outdoor variety.

sections.name: Best gifts for apartment scenes
sections.description: Beauty Kit, Laptop, Mirror, and Study Kit create frequent solo behavior inside apartments. The Laptop can attract up to 3 visitors and sometimes appears to crash.

sections.name: Best gifts for group moments
sections.description: CD (up to 4 Miis), Wii U (up to 4 Miis), Laptop (up to 3), and Rent-a-Cat Coupon all create shared comedy or friendship scenes on the island.

sections.name: Best gifts for outdoor variety
sections.description: Guitar, Fishing Pole, Metal Detector, Soccer Ball, Skateboard, and Baseball Bat add visible activity to outdoor locations and make your island map feel more alive.

sections.name: Best gift for fight recovery
sections.description: Punching Bag is the only level-up gift that angry Miis can use after a fight. It appears more often when the Mii is upset and is especially practical for drama-heavy islands.

growthMilestones: Give a Mii their first level-up gift
Unlock the Concert Hall with a song
Unlock the Observation Tower with a gift

tomodachiLifeClothingAndHats.title: Tomodachi Life Clothing and Hats Guide
tomodachiLifeClothingAndHats.intro: Clothing and hats are the core style systems for shaping how your island looks every day. Clothing totals 428 item types and 3,341 colors, while hats total 157 items and 1,111 colors.

priorities.name: Clothing Shop Unlock
priorities.description: Opens with 1 male + 1 female resident and 5 solved problems. Daily Specials refresh with up to 5 random outfits each day. Seasonal clothing rotates every 3 months.

priorities.name: Hats Shop Unlock
priorities.description: Opens with 2 male + 2 female residents and 10 solved problems. Shows 3 different hats daily. Bought hats stay available forever under In Stock.

priorities.name: Clothing Categories
priorities.description: 428 items total: Masculine (75), Feminine (109), Unisex (69), Formal Wear (69), Costumes (87). Once an outfit or color is acquired, it becomes permanently available.

priorities.name: Hat Categories
priorities.description: 157 items total: Regular hats (69), Costume hats (32), Hair accessories (56). Hats and outfits are separate layers — one hat can match many outfit combinations.

priorities.name: Clothing Reactions
priorities.description: Loved it: Mii spins in the air. Really likes it: jumps. Likes it: hands on hips. Does not like: tilts head. Does not like at all: looks away in embarrassment.

priorities.name: Import Wear and Seasonal Stock
priorities.description: Rare style unlocks come from special events, space travel, and Import Wear via StreetPass. Seasonal clothing rotates every 3 months for both shops.

tomodachiLifeInteriorsGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Interiors Guide
tomodachiLifeInteriorsGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life includes 102 interior designs in the main release. The Interiors shop unlocks once you have at least $200 in pocket money, and the game includes six level-up-only interiors that do not appear for normal sale.

achievements.title: Family
achievements.description: An ordinary family-style room. Exclusive to level-up rewards. Cannot be purchased from the normal Interiors shop.

achievements.title: Modern Asian
achievements.description: A clean, stylish room built around a shoes-off, carefully kept look. Exclusive to level-up rewards.

achievements.title: Natural Wood
achievements.description: A soothing wood-focused room, warm and timeless. Works well for relaxed or nature-themed islands.

achievements.title: Plant
achievements.description: A bright plant-heavy room built around greenery and an airy atmosphere. Good for cheerful, easygoing Miis.

achievements.title: Polka Dot
achievements.description: A lively room with bright polka-dot energy. Great for comedy islands and bold room variety.

achievements.title: Tiled
achievements.description: A tile-focused room with a distinctive clean pattern and strong visual identity. Exclusive to level-up rewards.

achievements.title: Fairy-tale Castle ($500)
achievements.description: A magical, high-concept room that turns an apartment into a fantasy castle scene. One of the most visually dramatic interiors.

achievements.title: How to Unlock the Interiors Shop
achievements.description: The Interiors Shop unlocks once you have at least $200 in pocket money across any two Miis.

tomodachiLifeTravelersAndStreetPass.title: Tomodachi Life Travelers and StreetPass Guide
tomodachiLifeTravelersAndStreetPass.intro: Travelers are special Miis that move between islands through StreetPass. The Port handles departures and arrivals, while the Campground is where incoming travelers stay for the night.

faqs.question: How do I create my own traveler in Tomodachi Life?
faqs.answer: Two married Miis must have a baby. Once that child grows up on day six, choose to send them away to travel instead of making them a resident. StreetPass must be enabled on both the system and for Tomodachi Life.

faqs.question: How do the Port and Campground unlock in Tomodachi Life?
faqs.answer: The Port unlocks after at least 1 StreetPass or after sending someone to travel. The Campground unlocks when you send or receive a traveler. The Campground can hold up to 10 tents at once.

faqs.question: What happens after a Tomodachi Life StreetPass tag?
faqs.answer: Each StreetPass can send 1 traveler from your Port queue. If the other island also has a traveler waiting, 1 can arrive on your island. The Port can report up to 10 arriving boats at a time and the dock holds up to 50 travelers total.

faqs.question: How do I find and help a newly arrived Tomodachi Life traveler?
faqs.answer: New travelers may appear at the fountain, park, observation tower, beach, café, or amusement park. Accept their request to camp. At 4:00 PM they retire to the Campground and set up a tent.

faqs.question: What problems can Tomodachi Life campground travelers have?
faqs.answer: Dirty travelers need a bath set. Homesick travelers need a music box or head pat. Hungry travelers need food or drinks. Stomach bugs need stomach medicine. Hot head needs cold medicine. Solved problems can earn special food for 1 to 5 Play Coins.

faqs.question: What rewards do Tomodachi Life travelers leave behind?
faqs.answer: Well-helped travelers may leave a letter and a treasure gift. Possible gifts include tissues, copper coin, silver coin, and gold coin. Arriving boats can also bring another island's Import Wear exclusive outfits and hats.

tomodachiLifeImportWear.title: Tomodachi Life Import Wear Guide
tomodachiLifeImportWear.intro: Import Wear is the island's special import shop. Instead of a simple daily rotation, it preserves imported stock from StreetPass exchanges and historical SpotPass distributions. It matters most for completionists.

faqs.question: How does Tomodachi Life Import Wear unlock?
faqs.answer: Import Wear opens when you first receive clothing through StreetPass or SpotPass, or when you first choose your island's export clothing via the export quiz after the Hats shop opens.

faqs.question: What does the Tomodachi Life Import Wear shop sell?
faqs.answer: Import Wear stocks clothing and hats brought in from other islands through StreetPass and from official SpotPass distribution. These are outfits and headwear that do not come from the normal shop rotation.

faqs.question: How do I choose my export outfit in Tomodachi Life?
faqs.answer: After the Hats shop opens, the export quiz offers three clothing options. You may retake the quiz one time, but once one export item is chosen, it cannot be changed. Your island only gets one permanent export outfit choice per save.

faqs.question: Does Tomodachi Life imported stock stay in the shop permanently?
faqs.answer: Yes. Your chosen export outfit travels to other islands through StreetPass and stays in their Import Wear catalog forever. Items you receive are likewise added as permanent shop stock once obtained.

faqs.question: What happened to SpotPass in Tomodachi Life?
faqs.answer: Community documentation records that SpotPass ended on April 8, 2024, with 24 SpotPass items documented historically. Modern completion runs rely on what is already stocked on a save file and on StreetPass-traded imports.

tomodachiLifeConcertHall.title: Tomodachi Life Concert Hall Songs
tomodachiLifeConcertHall.intro: The Concert Hall turns leveled-up Miis into performers. Once a Mii learns a song genre, you can rewrite lyrics, change outfits, build groups, and turn ordinary islanders into full comedy acts or parody bands.

settings.name: Song Types
settings.description: 8 song types: Metal, Pop, Rock and Roll, Rap, Ballad, Opera, Techno, and Musical. Default songs include Dragon's Fire (Metal), Loving Things (Pop), Livin' the Dream (Rap), and Reach for the Stars (Musical).

settings.name: Performance Customization
settings.description: You can change a song's lyrics and title, change the singer's outfit before the performance, and add extra Miis as background dancers. This makes Concert Hall a comedy tool as well as a music feature.

settings.name: Group Performances
settings.description: Groups can contain 2 to 7 Miis. Every Mii must know the same song to perform together. Some songs use one vocalist; others have all members sing. Up to 50 groups and 50 saved group songs are allowed.

settings.name: Why Concert Hall is Memorable
settings.description: Nintendo UK ran a 2015 Friendship Fiesta Concert Hall Contest built around player-made Tomodachi Life songs. Custom lyrics and bizarre performance energy made it one of the game's most shareable systems.

tomodachiLifeVoiceCatchphrases.title: Tomodachi Life Voice and Catchphrases Guide
tomodachiLifeVoiceCatchphrases.intro: Tomodachi Life sells the illusion of personality through text-to-speech, speech bubbles, and player-written phrases. A Mii's voice settings, spoken name data, catchphrase, and emotional lines all work together to make apartment dialogue feel personal.

entries.title: Voice sliders and preset voices
entries.description: 6 preset voices: male child, female child, male adult, female adult, elderly male, elderly female. Custom voices use 6 sliders: Pitch, Speed, Quality, Tone, Accent, and Intonation for full personality control.

entries.title: Name pronunciation system
entries.description: First and last name allow up to 15 written characters; nickname up to 10. Pronunciation can be entered separately from the visible name with up to 30 characters for first/last and 20 for nickname.

entries.title: Five phrase slots for everyday mood
entries.description: The five phrase types are Catchphrase, Happy phrase, Sad phrase, Angry phrase, and Worried phrase. A catchphrase is given as a level-up reward. The four emotional phrases are given when a Mii has a related problem state.

entries.title: Apartment speech bubbles
entries.description: When you enter a Mii's apartment, they may say their catchphrase, a random line, or a mood line. Tapping the speech bubble triggers a random dialogue line. A new bubble appears every hour.

tomodachiLifeUnlockGuide.title: Tomodachi Life Unlock Guide
tomodachiLifeUnlockGuide.intro: Tomodachi Life unlocks its island in layers. Population size, solved problems, money saved, level-up gifts, romance events, marriage, and StreetPass activity all push the island forward.

steps.title: Start with the built-in basics
steps.description: Mii Apartments, Beach, Town Hall, and Food Mart are available from the beginning. The Fountain unlocks through the tutorial sequence.

steps.title: Grow the island and solve problems
steps.description: Clothing Store: 1 male + 1 female resident + 5 solved problems. Hat Store: 2 male + 2 female + 10 problems. Mii News: 7 islanders with at least 3 of each gender.

steps.title: Use money and level-ups to expand
steps.description: Interiors Store: save over $200. Concert Hall: give a song to a leveled Mii. Observation Tower: give a gift to a leveled Mii. Rankings Board: 5+ Miis + 15 problems. Compatibility Tester: 2+2 Miis + 30 problems.

steps.title: Hit the midgame population milestone
steps.description: Photo Studio: 10+ islanders + 50 solved problems. Pawn Shop: obtain 5 different treasures. These open up key management and content tools for mid-game.

steps.title: Push romance forward
steps.description: Park, Amusement Park, and Café all unlock when an islander confesses love. These are among the most memorable social locations and unlock a wave of new events.

steps.title: Finish the family and travel layer
steps.description: Mii Homes: celebrate an islander wedding. Port: activate StreetPass + visit Hat Store. Import Wear: StreetPass or export quiz. Campground: send or receive a traveler.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Tomodachi Life

questions.question: What is Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream?
questions.answer: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is the upcoming Nintendo Switch sequel to the 3DS classic. It releases on April 16, 2026, supports up to 2 players, and includes a free downloadable demo available now.

questions.question: Is there a Tomodachi Life demo available?
questions.answer: Yes. A free demo for Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream is available to download on Nintendo Switch right now. The demo lets you start building your island before the full game launches.

questions.question: How many languages does Tomodachi Life support?
questions.answer: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream supports 10 languages: Japanese, English, French, German, Italian, Spanish, Korean, Dutch, Chinese Simplified, and Chinese Traditional.

questions.question: How do Mii personalities work in Tomodachi Life?
questions.answer: During Mii creation, four sliders place each resident into one of 16 personality types grouped as Easygoing, Outgoing, Independent, and Confident. Personalities affect social chemistry, dialogue flavor, and romance outcomes.

questions.question: How do I get QR codes for Tomodachi Life?
questions.answer: You can find Mii QR codes on MiiWiki's official archive, the Crystal Dreams community page, and Nintendo Life's curated packs. Scan them in Mii Maker, then import through Town Hall's New Mii option.

questions.question: What is the best beginner tip for Tomodachi Life?
questions.answer: Prioritize solving black-bubble problem requests as soon as they appear. Each solved problem unlocks new buildings, expands your island, and creates more social events between residents.

cta.title: Ready to Explore Tomodachi Life?
cta.description: Join the Tomodachi Life community on Reddit for Mii QR codes, tips, and discussions!

qr-codes.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life QR Codes - Import Miis & Collections
qr-codes.metaDescription: Find Tomodachi Life QR codes for Nintendo characters, celebrities, and fan-made Miis. Learn how to scan and import them into your island.
qr-codes.whatIsDescription: Mii QR codes let you import pre-made characters into Tomodachi Life through Mii Maker. Collections cover Nintendo staff, celebrities, anime, and original creations.

whySteps.step1Description: Import your favorite characters without rebuilding them by hand
whySteps.step2Description: Browse curated collections by theme, franchise, or community source
whySteps.step3Description: Populate a fresh island quickly with personality-ready Miis

personalities.title: Tomodachi Life Personalities
personalities.description: Complete guides to all 16 Tomodachi Life personality types, how the quiz works, and how personalities affect island life and social events.
personalities.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Personalities - All 16 Types Explained
personalities.metaDescription: Complete Tomodachi Life personality guide covering all 16 types, the personality quiz sliders, group traits, and how personalities affect friendships and romance.
personalities.whatIsDescription: Tomodachi Life uses four sliders to assign each Mii one of 16 personality types. Personalities shape dialogue, social chemistry, and key island outcomes.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn what each personality type looks and acts like on the island
whySteps.step2Description: Use personality compatibility to shape friendships and romances
whySteps.step3Description: Build a diverse island personality mix for more varied events

relationships.title: Tomodachi Life Relationships
relationships.description: Guides to friendships, romance, marriage, compatibility, and relationship status in Tomodachi Life.
relationships.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Relationships - Romance, Marriage & Friendship
relationships.metaDescription: Complete Tomodachi Life relationship guides covering friendship ladders, romance, compatibility testing, marriage, and how to protect bonds from breakups.
relationships.whatIsDescription: Guides to friendship status ladders, romance progression, compatibility testing, marriage steps, and managing breakups and fights in Tomodachi Life.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn the fastest ways to raise friendship and romance status
whySteps.step2Description: Understand the confession, dating, and marriage flow
whySteps.step3Description: Avoid arguments and breakups that can set back your island

locations.title: Tomodachi Life Locations
locations.description: Guides to every island location in Tomodachi Life including shops, social areas, and unlock requirements.
locations.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Locations - All Shops & Island Areas
locations.metaDescription: Complete guide to all Tomodachi Life island locations, unlock requirements, and what happens at each building and social area.
locations.whatIsDescription: Guides to every island building and area in Tomodachi Life, including shops, social hangouts, Mii Homes, and the unlock requirements for each.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn the exact conditions that open each new building
whySteps.step2Description: Understand what events and interactions each area supports
whySteps.step3Description: Build toward late-game areas like Mii Homes and the Port

food.title: Tomodachi Life Food
food.description: Food guides for Tomodachi Life covering the Food Mart rotation, favorite foods, reaction tiers, and collection basics.
food.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Food Guide - Favorites, Reactions & Collection
food.metaDescription: Complete Tomodachi Life food guide covering the Food Mart rotation, food reaction tiers, favorite and worst-ever foods, and collection tips.
food.whatIsDescription: Food guides cover the daily Food Mart rotation, reaction tiers from All-Time Favorite to Worst Ever, and collection strategies for the Town Hall food list.

whySteps.step1Description: Know which foods cheer up sad Miis in an emergency
whySteps.step2Description: Track All-Time Favorites and Worst Evers for every Mii
whySteps.step3Description: Use Daily Specials to grow your food catalog systematically

interiors.title: Tomodachi Life Interiors
interiors.description: Guides to all interior designs in Tomodachi Life including shop purchases, level-up exclusives, and apartment customization tips.
interiors.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Interiors - All 102 Rooms & Level-Up Exclusives
interiors.metaDescription: Complete Tomodachi Life interiors guide covering all 102 room designs, the 6 level-up-only exclusives, shop rotation, and apartment styling tips.
interiors.whatIsDescription: Interior guides cover all room designs available in Tomodachi Life, including the 6 exclusive level-up-only interiors and shop-bought options.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn which 6 interiors only come from level-up rewards
whySteps.step2Description: Match interior themes to Mii personalities and island stories
whySteps.step3Description: Prioritize room buys based on availability and visual impact

dreams.title: Tomodachi Life Dreams
dreams.description: Guides to sleep dreams, rare dream events, and dream types in Tomodachi Life.
dreams.metaTitle: Tomodachi Life Dreams - Rare Events & All Dream Types
dreams.metaDescription: Complete Tomodachi Life dreams guide covering all dream types, rare sleep events, how to trigger specific dreams, and what changes between versions.
dreams.whatIsDescription: Dream guides cover the sleep system in Tomodachi Life, including ordinary dream types, rare dreams, and how dream events vary by version.

whySteps.step1Description: Know which dream types are rare and what triggers them
whySteps.step2Description: Learn how the sleep and dream schedule works on your island
whySteps.step3Description: Track all dream categories for a complete island experience
